title: PFI Engine — Patellofemoral Instability Assessment
tagline: Automated quantitative assessment of patellofemoral instability from knee MRI.
disclaimer: FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY. Not for clinical diagnosis. Not FDA-cleared, not CE-marked. Data shown is from pre-computed test cases on the publicly available fastMRI dataset.
about: Pre-research-use prototype. Computes 14 literature-validated indices across patellar height, trochlear morphology, lateralization, and patellar tilt domains. Reports composite phenotype using cartilage-based MRI conventions per Tanaka 2023, Biedert 2006, and Pfirrmann 2000.
methodology_placeholder: Methodology details to appear with published validation paper [Skeletal Radiology, in preparation].
citations: Insall J, Salvati E. Patella position in the normal knee joint. Radiology 1971.
Caton J et al. Methodes de mesure de la hauteur de la rotule. Rev Chir Orthop 1982.
Biedert RM, Albrecht S. The patellotrochlear index. KSSTA 2006.
Carrillon Y et al. Patellar instability: assessment on MR images. Radiology 2000.
Pfirrmann CWA et al. Femoral trochlear dysplasia: MR findings. Radiology 2000.
Tanaka MJ et al. The cartilaginous trochlea in patients with patellofemoral instability. AJSM 2023.
Sallay PI et al. Acute dislocation of the patella. KSSTA 1996 / Brattström H 1964.
Hinckel BB et al. Patellar tendon-trochlear groove distance. JFM 2015.
Barbosa RM et al. Reliability of trochlear dysplasia indices on MRI. Skeletal Radiol 2024.

cases.headline: Normal
cases.summary: Healthy patellofemoral anatomy across all four domains.

cases.headline: Severe Alta
cases.summary: Patella alta confirmed across all three patellar-height indices.

cases.headline: Anatomic variant
cases.summary: Long non-articular patellar pole inflates IS index; CDI and PTI normal.

cases.headline: Severe PFI
cases.summary: Multi-domain PFI: alta + trochlear dysplasia + tilt + lateralization.

cases.headline: Early dysplasia
cases.summary: Cartilaginous trochlea dysplastic, underlying bone normal.

phenotype_recommendations.Normal: No PFI features identified. No specific imaging follow-up indicated.
phenotype_recommendations.Moderate: Multiple PFI features. MSK or sports-medicine consult suggested if symptomatic.
phenotype_recommendations.Severe: Severe PFI features across multiple domains. MSK fellowship referral and surgical workup consideration.
